key_insight: A vision-guided DDPG controller enables wall-climbing robots to adaptively track curved ship-hull weld seams despite curvature changes and wheel slippage.
engineering_relevance: The approach improves seam-following accuracy and control stability for automated welding inspection or repair on complex marine surfaces.
method: Train a YOLOv11-OBB model on custom ship-hull seam data for real-time seam state estimation.
Use DDPG to learn differential steering control for adaptive wall-climbing robot tracking.
Optimize reward weights with Bayesian optimization to improve policy performance.
evidence_signal: On a 20-meter hull seam, the method achieved under 2 cm lateral offset and under 5° heading error, outperforming traditional PID control.
watch_item: Performance depends on high-quality visual detection and may be sensitive to domain shifts such as lighting, surface wear, or unseen hull geometries.
paper_title: Research on motion control of weld seam tracking technology for wall-climbing robots based on DDPG algorithm
